```markdown
<!-- readme -->

## Welcome Andrapple1 Users 👋

Thanks for checking out Firefox-mobile! This file is a short, focused welcome for new contributors — the next steps to get started, what to expect, and where to find help.

### Quick start

- 1) Read the main repository README: `README.md` for high-level project goals and architecture.
- 2) Check the `LICENSE` for contribution and redistribution terms.
- 3) Open an issue for the bug or feature you'd like to work on, or browse existing issues labeled `good first issue` or `help wanted`.

### Before you code

- Fork the repo and create a topic branch with a descriptive name (for example, `feature/login-ui` or `fix/crash-on-start`).
- Link your PR to an issue when possible and include a short, clear description of the change.

### Contributor checklist

- [ ] I read `README.md` and the license.
- [ ] I opened/linked an issue describing my change (or confirmed one exists).
- [ ] My branch name follows the repo convention and is up-to-date with the main branch.
- [ ] I ran the project's tests locally (see `README.md` for test commands) and flagged any breaking changes.
- [ ] My PR includes screenshots (UI changes) or reproduction steps (bug fixes).

### Communication and help

- If you need help, open an issue and tag it `help wanted`, or leave a comment on a related issue/PR.
- For design or UX questions, include mockups or example screenshots.

### Review process

- PRs will be reviewed by maintainers. Expect constructive feedback and small requested changes.
- Keep PRs focused and small — it's easier and faster to review.

Thanks again for contributing — small contributions matter. If you want me to add a PR template, contributor guide, or automated checks, tell me what you'd like and I can create them.

```
